mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, PDGFR, and PKC signaling to reduce AML cell proliferation and survival
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; FDA-approved for FLT3-mutated AML

mechanism: Selectively inhibits FLT3 and AXL tyrosine kinases to block oncogenic signaling in AML
additional_notes: Also known as ASP2215; approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML

mechanism: Potent and selective FLT3 tyrosine kinase inhibitor, suppressing FLT3-ITD-driven AML cell growth
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; very high potency against FLT3-ITD AML including MOLM13 and MV4-11

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF, VEGFR, and PDGFR to block proliferative and survival pathways
additional_notes: Approved for several solid tumors (e.g., HCC, RCC); used off-label in FLT3-ITD AML

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferase, leading to DNA hypomethylation and reactivation of silenced genes
additional_notes: FDA-approved for MDS/AML; effects are time-dependent and can be cytostatic/cytotoxic

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and RNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferase, leading to global hypomethylation and altered gene expression
additional_notes: FDA-approved for MDS/AML; active as both a DNA and RNA antimetabolite

mechanism: Selectively binds and inhibits anti-apoptotic protein BCL-2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT-199; widely used in AML in combination with hypomethylating agents or low-dose cytarabine

mechanism: Inhibits multiple histone deacetylase (HDAC) isoforms, leading to increased histone acetylation and altered transcription
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; approved for multiple myeloma; epigenetic modulator

mechanism: Inhibits class I and II HDACs, increasing histone acetylation and inducing cell cycle arrest and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; approved for cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL)

mechanism: Selectively inhibits class I HDACs, leading to chromatin remodeling and induction of apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for T-cell lymphomas; strong pro-apoptotic effects in AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 kinases, reducing JAK-STAT signaling involved in proliferation and cytokine signaling
additional_notes: Approved for myelofibrosis, polycythemia vera, and GVHD; AML sensitivity is variable

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, SRC family kinases, and KIT to block oncogenic signaling pathways
additional_notes: Approved for CML and Ph+ ALL; activity in AML depends on SRC/KIT dependence

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, KIT, and PDGFR tyrosine kinases, reducing downstream proliferative signaling
additional_notes: CML/GIST drug; weaker activity in unselected AML unless KIT/PDGFR lesions present

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK), and at higher doses affects TEC/SRC family kinases, impairing survival signaling
additional_notes: Approved for multiple B-cell malignancies; AML effects often via off-target TEC/SRC inhibition

mechanism: Binds cereblon (CRBN) to induce degradation of IKZF1/3 and other substrates, modulating immune and tumor cell signaling
additional_notes: Thalidomide/lenalidomide class; lenalidomide preferred in vitro due to better solubility and potency; approved for myeloma and MDS del(5q)

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma; strong viability effects in AML lines

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome chymotrypsin-like activity, inducing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; low- to mid-nM activity in AML cell lines

mechanism: Allosterically inhibits mTORC1, reducing protein synthesis and cell growth signaling
additional_notes: Also known as RAD001; approved for several solid tumors; effects in AML often more cytostatic

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, VEGFR, and PDGFR among other RTKs, blocking proliferation and angiogenic signaling
additional_notes: Approved for RCC and GIST; reported activity in FLT3-ITD AML

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and KIT, reducing oncogenic kinase signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML; AML activity is modest unless specific KIT/ABL lesions are present
